executive_summary: This assessment covers a cloud-deployed, external-facing web service that processes PII and integrates with third-party vendors. The system requires strong identity controls, comprehensive logging, application security testing, network segmentation, and certificate management. Container workloads add native firewall and scanning requirements. Several components are not triggered due to the absence of Windows infrastructure and privileged access patterns.

components.reasoning: Based on SCOPE-01 indicating an external-facing service and HYGN-03 confirming network access point requirements, the system must integrate with the corporate network access point infrastructure.
SCOPE-05 confirms the service processes user events and HYGN-08 mandates centralised logging. All external-facing services require application-level logging forwarded to the SIEM.
SCOPE-01 identifies this as a new production service requiring Archer GRC registration per governance policy. GOVN-01 confirms no existing Archer record.
HYGN-08 confirms log forwarding to the SIEM platform and LOG-03 indicates no existing ArcSight alert rules exist for this service. A new alert request is required.
SCOPE-03 confirms the service runs in containerised workloads (Kubernetes). Container-native firewall policies are mandatory per platform standards for all container deployments.
SCOPE-03 confirms container-based deployment. Automated container image scanning for vulnerabilities and misconfigurations is required before production deployment.
SCOPE-07 confirms the service has no privileged endpoints requiring endpoint privilege management. No user workstations are enrolled. CyberArk EPM is not applicable.
SCOPE-01 confirms an external-facing web application. HYGN-06 confirms no previous DAST has been performed. Dynamic application security testing is mandatory before go-live.
DATA-01 confirms the system stores PII in a relational database. HYGN-08 mandates database-level audit logging forwarded to the SIEM for all PII-processing systems.
SCOPE-04 indicates a fully managed cloud PaaS deployment with no direct filesystem access. File integrity monitoring is not applicable for this deployment model.
SCOPE-01 confirms an external-facing service and NET-01 indicates the correct DMZ placement has not yet been validated. Network segmentation review is required before production.
VENDOR-01 confirms third-party vendor integration for payment processing. OneTrust supplier security assessment is mandatory for all third-party vendors with data access.
SCOPE-03 confirms Linux-based container hosts. Unix OS-level audit logging must be configured and forwarded to the central SIEM per platform standards.
SCOPE-03 confirms exclusively Linux/container-based infrastructure. No Windows servers are present. Windows OS logging is not applicable.
SCOPE-01 confirms an external-facing service processing PII. ASSUR-01 confirms no penetration test has been performed. Penetration testing is mandatory before go-live for external-facing services.
IDM-01 confirms no privileged account usage patterns that require CyberArk PAM integration. Service uses standard OAuth2 for internal API calls only.
SCOPE-08 confirms the service makes outbound calls to external payment APIs. NET-03 indicates no outbound proxy has been defined. Proxy configuration is required.
SCOPE-01 identifies a new external-facing service processing PII with third-party integrations. GOVN-02 confirms no formal risk assessment has been completed. A full risk assessment is mandatory.
ASSUR-03 confirms no static application security testing has been run on the codebase. SCOPE-01 confirms a new custom application. SAST is required before production deployment.
VENDOR-01 confirms a third-party vendor handles payment processing. SCOPE-08 confirms external data sharing. The Supplier Cyber Security questionnaire is required for this vendor relationship.
SCOPE-01 confirms a new external-facing service. GOVN-03 confirms the SbD assessment has not yet been initiated. This assessment is mandatory for all new services in scope.
ASSUR-04 confirms the application uses open-source dependencies. No SCA scan has been performed per ASSUR-05. SCA is required to identify third-party component vulnerabilities.
NET-01 confirms the service will be network-accessible. ASSUR-06 confirms no Tenable scan has been scheduled. An authenticated vulnerability scan is required before production.
SCOPE-01 confirms an external-facing web application. NET-05 confirms no WAF is currently in place. WAF integration is mandatory for all externally exposed web services.
SCOPE-01 confirms a new external-facing HTTPS service. CERT-01 confirms no TLS certificate has been requested. A valid certificate must be obtained before the service goes live.
